definition: Operating business survived with meaningful unit/platform continuity; best analogue for live distressed acquisition.
Separable service/regulated/customer-file business survived while parent/banner failed.
Assets survived by being absorbed into a stronger strategic/platform buyer; often good lesson, not standalone turnaround.
Specific assets/customer files transferred to incumbent; banner not preserved.
Brand/IP survives mainly through licensing/portfolio model; original operating company usually dead.
Ongoing/watch case or continuation without enough proof of durable turnaround.
Continued in some form but proof is incomplete; needs survival verification before use.
Hybrid/unclear relaunch or absorption; useful only with explicit caveats.
Stores/operating engine liquidated; brand/domain/customer file bought cheap; weak standalone odds.
Legal/brand continuation but weak operating evidence, contraction, or unclear economics.
Repeat bankruptcy/distress after prior restructuring; strong warning that prior emergence was not success.
Operating company shut down; little durable brand/platform value evident.
